At Jacobs, we’re challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world’s most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.

factors.summary: The backlog orders have increased, primarily driven by new awards and acquisitions in the CMS and P&PS segments. While the CMS backlog growth was mainly due to the acquisition of KeyW, the P&PS segment saw an increase from new awards in the U.K. and U.S. markets. Additionally, the overall growth in revenues across these segments supports the increasing backlog.

evidence.text: The increase in backlog in Critical Mission Solutions (CMS) from December 28, 2018 was primarily the result of the acquisition of KeyW.
The increase in backlog in People & Places Solutions (P&PS) from December 28, 2018 was primarily the result of new awards in the U.K. and U.S. markets.
Revenues for the three months ended December 27, 2019 were $3.36 billion, an increase of $0.28 billion, or 9.0%, from $3.08 billion for the corresponding period last year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Backlog orders increased due to new awards and acquisitions, indicating strong demand.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing organic growth, particularly in the People & Places Solutions segment, driven by new awards in the U.K. and U.S. markets. This growth is supported by a three-year accelerated profitable growth strategy focused on innovation and transformation, resulting in a 9.0% revenue increase from $3.08 billion to $3.36 billion.

evidence.text: The increase in backlog in People & Places Solutions (P&PS) from December 28, 2018 was primarily the result of new awards in the U.K. and U.S. markets.

After spending three years transforming our portfolio and setting the foundation to get us where we are today, we launched a three-year accelerated profitable growth strategy at our Investor Day in February 2019, focused on innovation and continued transformation to build upon our position as the leading solutions provider for our clients.
Revenues for the three months ended December 27, 2019 were $3.36 billion, an increase of $0.28 billion, or 9.0%, from $3.08 billion for the corresponding period last year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth was driven by new awards and a strategic focus on innovation, leading to revenue increases.

Net earnings attributable to Jacobs from continuing operations for the fiscal quarter ended December 27, 2019 were $179.4 million (or $1.33 per diluted share), an increase of $114.5 million, or 176.5%, from $64.9 million (or $0.45 per diluted share) for the corresponding period last year.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is positive as both gross and operating margins are expanding. This improvement is attributed to the growth in higher-margin segments and effective cost management.

evidence.text: Gross profit for the first quarter of 2020 was $644.6 million, an increase of $76.1 million, or 13.4% from $568.5 million from the corresponding period last year.
Our gross profit margins were 19.2% and 18.4% for the three month periods ended December 27, 2019 and December 28, 2018, respectively.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Both gross and operating margins are expanding, indicating strong cost management and growth in high-margin segments.

factors.summary: The company has implemented restructuring and efficiency programs that have resulted in measurable savings. However, SG&A expenses have increased moderately, driven by factors such as the KeyW acquisition, higher restructuring charges, and personnel-related expenses.

evidence.text: The text does not discuss restructuring, cost reduction, or efficiency programs and their outcomes.

SG&A expenses for the three months ended December 27, 2019 were $493.2 million, an increase of $37.8 million, or 8.3%, from $455.4 million for the corresponding period last year. The increase in SG&A expenses as compared to the corresponding period last year was due primarily to incremental SG&A from the KeyW acquisition, higher restructuring charges and higher personnel-related and professional services expenses.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While restructuring programs have resulted in savings, the increase in SG&A expenses presents a mixed picture.

Net earnings attributable to Jacobs from continuing operations for the fiscal quarter ended December 27, 2019 were $179.4 million (or $1.33 per diluted share), an increase of $114.5 million, or 176.5%, from $64.9 million (or $0.45 per diluted share) for the corresponding period last year.

factors.summary: The company achieves differentiation through a combination of deep global domain expertise and cutting-edge technology. Proprietary technologies like TrackRecord and AquaDNA further strengthen its competitive position, enhancing efficiency and predictability in delivering solutions.

evidence.text: The Company’s deep global domain knowledge - applied together with the latest advances in technology - are why customers large and small choose to partner with Jacobs.

A strong foundation of data-rich innovative solutions is woven into every project that we deliver. These solutions employ an array of technical expertise to enable the most efficient, effective and predictable solutions for our customers, such as our proprietary technology software. Examples of these technologies include: TrackRecord, AquaDNA, Travel Service Optimisation (TSO), SafetyWeb, ProjectMapper, Flood Modeller, and Replica™.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong differentiation through proprietary technologies and global domain expertise provides a significant competitive advantage.

Revenues for the three months ended December 27, 2019 were $3.36 billion, an increase of $0.28 billion, or 9.0%, from $3.08 billion for the corresponding period last year. The increase in revenues for the three month period year over year was due in part to fiscal 2020 incremental revenues from the June 2019 KeyW acquisition in addition to overall growth in our CMS and P&PS businesses.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in customer diversification is suggested, but lack of specific information leaves some uncertainty.

factors.summary: Digital automation is a significant focus for the company, particularly in enhancing cybersecurity and data analytics through acquisitions like KeyW, and improving internal efficiency and competitive advantage via internally developed technologies such as Replica™.

evidence.text: With our recent acquisition of KeyW, CMS offers a full suite of cyber services for its government and commercial clients, including defensive cyber operations and training, offensive cyber operations, cloud and data analytics, threat intelligence, intelligence analysis, incident response and forensics, software and infrastructure security engineering, computer forensics and exploitation and information technology-operational technology (IT-OT) convergence services.

Replica™ is Jacobs’ digital twin solution software platform and consists of the following capabilities: Replica Parametric Design™, Replica Preview™, Replica Systems Analysis™, Replica Process™, Replica Hydraulics™, Replica Controls™, and Replica Air™. These technologies provide comprehensive simulation and analysis capabilities, enhancing project efficiency and decision-making.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant focus on digital automation through acquisitions and internal technologies enhances competitive advantage.

factors.summary: Volume trends are positive across both the CMS and P&PS segments, with both showing significant revenue growth. The growth appears to be driven by real demand rather than price increases.

evidence.text: Revenues for the three months ended December 27, 2019 were $3.36 billion, an increase of $0.28 billion, or 9.0%, from $3.08 billion for the corresponding period last year.
Revenues for the CMS segment for the three months ended December 27, 2019 were $1.18 billion, an increase of $147.4 million, or 14.2%, from $1.04 billion for the corresponding period last year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive volume trends across segments with significant revenue growth driven by real demand.

factors.summary: The company has been actively engaged in acquisition activity, with recent purchases including KeyW and Wood Group’s nuclear business, and another pending acquisition of John Wood Group's Nuclear consulting business expected to close by the end of the second fiscal quarter of 2020. These activities position the company as a leader in high-value government services and technology-enabled solutions, enhancing its portfolio with intellectual property-driven technology and strengthening its position as a Tier-1 global nuclear services provider.

evidence.text: Our acquisitions of KeyW and Wood Group’s nuclear business will further position us as a leader in high-value government services and technology-enabled solutions, enhancing our portfolio by adding intellectual property-driven technology with unique proprietary C5ISR (command, control, communications, computer, combat systems, intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance) rapid solutions, and amplifying Jacobs’ position as a Tier-1 global nuclear services provider.

On August 20, 2019, Jacobs announced that it had entered into an agreement to acquire John Wood Group's Nuclear consulting, remediation and program management business. The transaction is expected to close by the end of fiscal 2020 second quarter.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active acquisition strategy positions the company as a leader and strengthens its portfolio and market position.

factors.summary: The end markets, particularly Critical Mission Solutions (CMS) and People & Places Solutions (P&PS), are experiencing strong growth. CMS showed a significant revenue increase of 14.2%, while P&PS also grew by 6.3%, driven by investments in sectors like Advanced Facilities, water, and transport infrastructure.

evidence.text: Revenues for the CMS segment for the three months ended December 27, 2019 were $1.18 billion, an increase of $147.4 million, or 14.2%, from $1.04 billion for the corresponding period last year.

Revenues for the People & Places Solutions (P&PS) segment for the three months ended December 27, 2019 were $2.18 billion, an increase of $128.8 million, or 6.3%, from $2.05 billion for the corresponding period last year. The increases in revenue were due in part to portfolio growth across our businesses, highlighted by strong investment in Advanced Facilities, water and transport infrastructure and project management/construction management (PMCM) sectors.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The end markets CMS and P&PS are showing strong growth with significant revenue increases.
